title: feat: add test for nested stream and check value has predict_id
body:
This PR is to add test for nested streaming where we have an agent streaming its response and its tools to run some dspy programs.

- I added the test to validate the use case
- I add check the predict_id before attempt to response to client

Related issue: https://github.com/stanfordnlp/dspy/issues/8454

cc @chenmoneygithub, @okhat

step_plan.summary: Add a regression test for nested streaming (agent streams while invoking streaming sub-program/tools) and harden the streaming pipeline so chunks missing or lacking a mapped `predict_id` no longer crash streaming to clients.

steps.operation: Harden listener-routing logic to tolerate ModelResponseStream chunks without a routable predict_id
steps.reason: Nested streaming can emit ModelResponseStream chunks where `predict_id` is missing/None or not present in `predict_id_to_listener`; directly indexing `predict_id_to_listener[value.predict_id]` risks KeyError/AttributeError and breaks the SSE stream.
steps.side_effects: May change behavior for “unroutable” chunks (they will be yielded directly or ignored per chosen fallback); downstream listeners may receive fewer callbacks in these edge cases; should not affect non-nested streaming but could alter ordering/visibility of some chunks if previously crashing.

steps.operation: Add a defensive predict_id check when converting streamed chunks into client-facing streaming responses
steps.reason: The PR notes a need to “check the predict_id before attempt to response to client”; ensuring `streaming_response` can serialize/forward chunks even when upstream metadata like `predict_id` is missing prevents client-stream failure at the API boundary in nested streaming scenarios.
steps.side_effects: May alter SSE output for edge-case chunks (e.g., passing through raw deltas without routing metadata); could require updating/relaxing assertions in existing streaming tests if they assumed predict_id is always present.
